scores.label: variant 1
scores.note: Hammers exact-substring fidelity with a 'mental Ctrl+F' verification step and adds explicit character-budget math to pull both ratios down into target ranges.

scores.label: variant 2
scores.note: Restructures as numbered hard-constraint blocks with explicit ratio targets and a 'shorten until verbatim' fallback rule to lift exact-match rates and pull HL ratio under 25%.

scores.label: variant 3
scores.note: Frames constraints as 'two inviolable rules' with a 'shorten until verbatim' fallback and explicit compression recipe (agent/verb/number/negation) to fix exact-match failures and tighten highlight ratios.

scores.label: variant 1
scores.note: Adds explicit smart-quote/em-dash preservation warnings with a worked example, and replaces vague length guidance with a 3-step measure-draft-cut procedure to fix the dominant failure modes (44-55% exact match, ratio overruns to 0.98).

scores.label: variant 2
scores.note: Restructures both prompts into numbered ABSOLUTE RULES with explicit Unicode codepoint warnings (U+2019) and a budget-stress-test example, directly attacking the two failure modes — non-verbatim copies (especially around curly quotes) and runaway ratios up to 0.98.

scores.label: variant 3
scores.note: Frames the rules as machine-checkable predicates (`s in body == True`, explicit U+2019 vs U+0027) with ✓/✗ tables and a verify-then-cut procedure, plus a curly-quote example mirroring the actual failed Arctic card to teach the model not to silently normalize Unicode.
